item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I rather enjoyed the concept of "goofy weatherman predicts the weather, no matter what logic dictates," although I could have done without the more literal takes on the figures of speech. Really, really could have done without the ending, though. I think it's just an issue I have with the trope of "GOD-LIKE BEINGS - THEY'RE JUST LIKE US!" that pops up here periodically.

Still, it walked a fine line between goofy and creepy, and that's always fun.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"which was discovered to occur only as a rare occurrence."

pls

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I saw this image on the Scarfolk Council website and just had to share:

+ Tomorrow's Forecast
- Lack of Vision in some areas

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Doctor Fitzpatrick: Okay, SCP-2049-1.

SCP-2049-1: …who?

This is awful. Researchers call contained sapient objects "SCP-number" instead of real names and nicknames as a psychological trick to not get attached. They don't tell random people they try to make contact with "okay, ess see pee two thousand forty nine dash one" and they certainly don't stop contact with "that will be all" without any means to re-establish it. That goes beyond lolfoundation.

Downvote until it's fixed, obviously.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel kinda bad for this guy. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's over a damn small area, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really enjoyed this, but I think what really made this for me was the name for your MTF. I think I enjoyed that way to much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Keter is both. Furthermore, according to Foundation pinciples and beliefs, the shattering of the perception of normalcy is considered a threat to the Foundation and Humanity at-large. Humanity panics as they realize reality has no innate rhyme or reason. Society breaks down, and peope violently turn on the Foundation and other GoIs out of fear or anger.

Or so the Foundation believes. They, uh, don't exactly have the brightest view on humanity at large. Or they know exactly what will happen when humanity understands that Eldritch Abominations are a dime a dozen, and there's nothing they can really do about it.

Fundimentally, this SCP can't be effectively (actively) contained. There is literally nothing the Foundation can do to stop the phenomenon itself from occuring, so containment is all about covering up the aftermath. Because this sort of thing will ruin the perception of normalcy. All it takes is a few failed coverups to cause a worldwide media fenzy, people start paying attention to the cracks of reality and find more weird stuff, and then society collapses as it tries to adapt to a "new" normal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I do not see why this is classified as Keter. We have examples of SCPs that are both Euclid and hard to contain. Keters are mainly defined by being innately highly threatening to the Foundation and/or humanity as a whole, as described in Object Classes; the difficulty of containment is more or less secondary in importance to this, and not essential to being classified Keter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not keen at all on the MTF name, but the rest of it slowly charmed me. I'd like a little more detail on the forecasts, and maybe a few more, but I am a sucker for variations-on-a-theme SCPs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This reminds of Nightvale in a good way. It's strange and weird. I loved it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As I told ya a few days ago in that one chat thinger, I think this one's quite awesome. IMO it's rather challenging to create a credible Keter skip that's genuinely entertaining as well as amusing. You executed this beautifully - not only did I end up finding it quite funny but I also ended up feeling a little sorry for K… K…. uh let's settle for Weather Guy, shall we? Either way - upvoticle.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is nothing like most of the other SCPs…

I love it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I believe it originates from nowhere, or both places. The clouds are negatively charged, the ground is positively charged (or vice versa), they get attracted to each other and BANG! Lightning.

So, it comes from both places, the positive charge and negative charge meet in the middle.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Daww, poor … guy? Thing? Whatever. +1 so it'll feel beter

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, this was an interesting read, as well as a unique concept for an SCP. I'll give you an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
John Cleese as Kic, undoubtedly.

This is good. Keeps surprising the reader:
Oh no, weather forecast in the UK, not ag… oh. Yeah. Not any forecast…
Who cares about weather on other planets?… Oh. It happens here too.
and it keeps up all the way through, in one of the best examples of British nonsense I've ever read.

Take my +1 with ribbons, feathers and flowers, Author! ;>

item.title: Staff Post: Reversion Notice
item.content:encoded: Version 10 of this article has been reverted, as it made an unnecessary wording change.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this falls too much on the side of silly for my taste, I would prefer it if all the weather was actually physically possible on other planets, and if Fitzpatrick didn't slip up and tell an uncontained alien about the Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Okay MTF 294-Samekh what would you like you're name to be?
"We get to choose our own name?"
"Okay if you're sure…"
"Wait what?"
"Done, your name is official."
"NOOOOOOOOOOOO!"

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At first, I was confused. Then I started laughing. Then I got to the final interview, and now I'm left with the image of the reporter sitting alone in his dingy station, dreaming about reporting the weather…

Take my upvote while I sob bitter tears over my computer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can totally see this on an episode of Rick And Morty.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Depends on the polarities and your definition of positive current. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lightning#Cloud_to_ground_.28CG.29

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked the story, but there are too many strange artistic choices for me to upvote.

+ Such as...
– hide block

MTF 294-Samekh ("We Get To Choose Our Own Name?") will be deployed to Manningtree.

Call me boring and lame, but the idea that this would make it all the way to a final publication by The Foundation on a keter skip baffles me.

A single, spiral shaped cloud manifests over an area directly west of Manningtree, with a 1km diameter. High levels of gamma radiation exist in this zone until the weather dissipates. Protons are detected travelling at high velocity within the zone, suggesting a Hadron collider-like effect. Cloud spirals upward, seemingly unaffected by winds, into the high troposphere. Anomalously bright lightning also occurs during this event.

Nothing exactly wrong, but the whole thing is worded weirdly. Also, it told me a lot of things that I didn't care about and didn't say a lot of things that I wanted to know. Did the population of Manningtree show a spike in cancer diagnoses?

Doctor Fitzpatrick: Okay, SCP-2049-1.
SCP-2049-1: …who?

What was the purpose of this exchange? I see no reason for Doctor Fitzpatrick to say this.

It's a no-vote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

Great stuff!

+ Show whatever
- Hide whatever

I liked the MTF Name, I was prepared to up vote during the logs, and the interview was just icing on the cake. I love "unintentionally interdimensional" skips.

I will reiterate that lightning does strike from both the ground and the sky on a regular basis.

Okay, SCP-2049-1

I don't know why but this always breaks immersion for me. I just can't see anyone saying something with NINE syllables (or ten? Do they say the dash?) every time they need to refer to their subject.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the implication that the Foundation watching the broadcasts is why they still happen.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think you quite read up on the reason behind classifications.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It just doesnt seem keter to me. It only happens in one city, and they get an at least vague forewarning of when it will happen. There are other scp's where they have contained full cities, and this one just seems lackluster. its good enough to +1, but i hope someone makes it euclid

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the mix of weird-but-not-randomness to this combined with the genuine emotion involved. definitely +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A thunderstorm manifests, which causes lightning to originate from the ground and strike in the thunderstorm itself.

Isn't that just normal lightning?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bit of an old post, but it's because the weather itself is anomalous and possibly dangerous and there's no real way to stop it, especially with the reporter guy getting fired so they may not be able to track it. Get a few curious reporters that the Foundation misses lingering, and…yeah. Overall this is probably one of the hardest SCPs the Foundation has to contain.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why is this Keter?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah, you beautiful person. This is why I fell in love with the SCP Foundation, for writing like this. Weird and fun and mysterious, shimmering just on the edge of the creepy otherworldliness that you see in a Tool music video. This has a hook alright, and it sink right into the flesh of my cheek and dragged me in.

Naturally, you get a +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ;~; Poor ol guy, he just wants to report the weather. Here, take my donations +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I first noticed this a while ago, but didn't give it a proper read-through till now. I'm glad I have. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A thunderstorm manifests, which causes lightning to originate from the ground and strike in the thunderstorm itself.

… I… thought that was the normal thing. The electricity comes from earth and goes up… right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really love the MTF name, and the final interview is keeping me from a downvote, but overall I don't find this too interesting. Most of all, could you please identify where Manningtree is?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
<An animal-like whimper can be heard through the telephone.>

That cinched it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed a mispelling of Celsius per te.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually, this is the only bit that is too much for me, but the rest hits me in the adequate weirdness and feels spots. Have an upvote. :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not a fan of this. There's a number of small errors, such as that Manningtree should be more clearly stated to be in the UK and that the footnotes are at times misused, but overall there's nothing in this to make it work. The stuff with him being fired and depressed plays out obviously, doesn't add much, and feels tacked on to the concept.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
MTF-294 Samekh ("We Get To Choose Our Own Name?")

If that's a dumb reason to upvote, I don't care.
